---
title: "CNC_UK"
output: html_document
---

This document runs the CNC UK analysis for Morgan et al. 2026 - "The City 
Nature Challenge increases urban biodiversity knowledge and public 
engagement with blue-green spaces"

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
library(readr)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(stringr)
library(tidyverse)
library(lubridate)
library(ggplot2)
library(patchwork)
library(DHARMa)
library(lme4)
library(lmerTest)
library(dplyr)
library(purrr)
library(tibble)
library(vroom)
library(stringr)
```

Import datasets after you have run 1_File_Merge
```{r import 25 cities for analysis, include=FALSE}
inat_hull <- read_csv("hull_all.csv")
inat_birm <- read_csv("birmingham_all.csv")
inat_liver <- read_csv("liverpool_all.csv")
inat_brist <- read_csv("bristol_all.csv")
inat_brig <- read_csv("brighton_all.csv")
inat_staff <- read_csv("stafford_all.csv")
inat_chesh <- read_csv("cheshire_all.csv")
inat_york <- read_csv("york_all.csv")
inat_northeast <- read_csv("northeast_all.csv")
inat_london <- read_csv("london_all.csv")
inat_man <- read_csv("manchester_all.csv")
inat_lanc <- read_csv("lancashire_all.csv")
inat_oxf <- read_csv("oxfordshire_all.csv")
inat_ed <- read_csv("edinburgh_all.csv")
inat_war_sol <- read_csv("war_sol_all.csv")
inat_suffolk <- read_csv("suffolk_all.csv")
inat_belfast <- read_csv("belfast_all.csv")
inat_berk <- read_csv("berkshire_all.csv")
inat_swansea <- read_csv("swansea_all.csv")
inat_dundee <- read_csv("dundee_all.csv")
inat_gloucest <- read_csv("gloucestershire_all.csv")
inat_coventry <- read_csv("coventry_all.csv")
inat_cardnew <- read_csv("cardiff_new_all.csv")
inat_nott <- read_csv("nott_all.csv")
inat_glas <- read_csv("glasgow_all.csv")
inat_urb <- read_csv("urbanness_top25_r.csv")
```

```{r unique users total}
# create list
inat_list <- list(
  Hull = inat_hull,
  Birmingham = inat_birm,
  Liverpool = inat_liver,
  Bristol = inat_brist,
  Brighton = inat_brig,
  Staffordshire = inat_staff,
  Cheshire = inat_chesh,
  York = inat_york,
  NorthEast = inat_northeast,
  London = inat_london,
  Manchester = inat_man,
  Lancashire = inat_lanc,
  Oxfordshire = inat_oxf,
  Edinburgh = inat_ed,
  WarwickandSolihull = inat_war_sol,
  Suffolk = inat_suffolk,
  Belfast = inat_belfast,
  Berkshire = inat_berk,
  Swansea = inat_swansea,
  Dundee = inat_dundee,
  Gloucestershire = inat_gloucest,
  Coventry = inat_coventry,
  CardiffNewport = inat_cardnew,
  Nottingham = inat_nott,
  Glasgow = inat_glas
)

# Summary of Users
user_summary <- map_dfr(
  inat_list,
  ~ tibble(
      unique_users = n_distinct(.x$user_id)
    ),
  .id = "city"
)
user_stats <- user_summary %>%
  summarise(
    total_users = sum(unique_users),
    mean_users = mean(unique_users),
    median_users = median(unique_users),
    max_users = max(unique_users),
    min_users = min(unique_users)
  )
user_stats
```

```{r record licenses}
# Create summary by city
license_summary <- map_dfr(
  inat_list,
  ~ tibble(
      license = .x$license
    ),
  .id = "city"
)

usable_licenses <- c(
  "CC0", "CC-BY", "CC-BY-NC", 
  "CC-BY-SA", "CC-BY-ND", 
  "CC-BY-NC-ND", "CC-BY-NC-SA"
)

open_licenses <- c("CC0", "CC-BY", "CC-BY-NC")

license_flags <- license_summary %>%
  mutate(
    usable = license %in% usable_licenses,
    open   = license %in% open_licenses
  )

overall_usable_pct <- license_flags %>%
  count(usable) %>%
  mutate(percent = n / sum(n) * 100)

overall_open_pct <- license_flags %>%
  count(open) %>%
  mutate(percent = n / sum(n) * 100)

usable_pct_by_city <- license_flags %>%
  group_by(city, usable) %>%
  summarise(n = n(), .groups = "drop_last") %>%
  mutate(percent = n / sum(n) * 100)

open_pct_by_city <- license_flags %>%
  group_by(city, open) %>%
  summarise(n = n(), .groups = "drop_last") %>%
  mutate(percent = n / sum(n) * 100)

license_breakdown <- license_summary %>%
  count(license) %>%
  mutate(percent = n / sum(n) * 100)

# Sanity check of one known blank license (authour knows this user has no
# licences set)
user_records_hull <- inat_hull %>% dplyr::filter(user_id == 6464285)
```

```{r process data and run SAC}
# Data cleaning with function
process_inat_city <- function(df, city_name) {
  
  message("Processing ", city_name, "...")
  
  # Filter and clean names (only take species level and remove subspecies name)
  df_clean <- df %>%
    # Remove blank or missing licenses
    filter(!is.na(license) & str_trim(license) != "") %>%
    filter(quality_grade == "research") %>%
    mutate(
      scientific_name = if_else(
        str_detect(scientific_name, "×"),
        scientific_name,
        word(scientific_name, 1, 2)
      )
    ) %>%
    drop_na(scientific_name)
  
  ##Diagnostic check 
  name_words <- str_count(df$scientific_name, "\\S+")
  more_than_two <- df$scientific_name[name_words > 2]
  
  message(" - ", length(unique(more_than_two)),
          " names had more than two words (subspecies or variants).")
  if (length(unique(more_than_two)) > 0) {
    message("   Example (up to 10): ",
            paste(head(unique(more_than_two), 10), collapse = "; "))
  }
  message(" - Retained unique names (simplified): ",
          length(unique(df_clean$scientific_name)))
  
  # Add date/month and year
  df_clean <- df_clean %>%
    mutate(
      date = as.Date(observed_on),
      month = floor_date(date, "month")
    ) %>%
    filter(date >= as.Date("2020-10-01") &
             date <= as.Date("2025-10-01")) %>%
    mutate(sep_year = year(date + months(3)))
  
  # Compute SAC WITH CNC
  sac_df <- df_clean %>%
    arrange(date) %>%
    group_by(month) %>%
    summarise(month_species = list(unique(scientific_name)), .groups = "drop") %>%
    mutate(
      cum_species = map_dbl(
        seq_along(month_species),
        ~ length(unique(unlist(month_species[1:.x])))
      )
    )
  
  # Remove CNC dates
  cnc_ranges <- list(
    "2021" = as.Date(c("2021-04-30", "2021-05-03")),
    "2022" = as.Date(c("2022-04-29", "2022-05-02")),
    "2023" = as.Date(c("2023-04-28", "2023-05-01")),
    "2024" = as.Date(c("2024-04-26", "2024-04-29")),
    "2025" = as.Date(c("2025-04-25", "2025-04-28"))
  )
  
  df_no_cnc <- df_clean %>%
    filter(
      !(date >= cnc_ranges[["2021"]][1] & date <= cnc_ranges[["2021"]][2]) &
      !(date >= cnc_ranges[["2022"]][1] & date <= cnc_ranges[["2022"]][2]) &
      !(date >= cnc_ranges[["2023"]][1] & date <= cnc_ranges[["2023"]][2]) &
      !(date >= cnc_ranges[["2024"]][1] & date <= cnc_ranges[["2024"]][2]) &
      !(date >= cnc_ranges[["2025"]][1] & date <= cnc_ranges[["2025"]][2])
    )
  
  # SAC without CNC
  sac_no_cnc <- df_no_cnc %>%
    arrange(date) %>%
    group_by(month) %>%
    summarise(month_species = list(unique(scientific_name)), .groups = "drop") %>%
    mutate(
      cum_species = map_dbl(
        seq_along(month_species),
        ~ length(unique(unlist(month_species[1:.x])))
      )
    )
  
  # Merge and calculate difference 
  merged_df <- merge(sac_df, sac_no_cnc, by = "month", suffixes = c("_with", "_without"))
  merged_df$diff <- merged_df$cum_species_with - merged_df$cum_species_without
  
  # Plot 
  p <- ggplot(merged_df, aes(x = month)) +
    geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = cum_species_without, ymax = cum_species_with),
                fill = "orange", alpha = 0.2) +
    geom_line(aes(y = cum_species_with), color = "orange", size = 0.8, linetype = "dashed") +
    geom_line(aes(y = cum_species_without), color = "steelblue", size = 0.8) +
    labs(
      x = "Month", y = "Cumulative Species Richness",
      title = paste("Species Accumulation Curve:", city_name, "– With vs Without CNC")
    ) +
    theme_minimal() +
    scale_x_date(date_labels = "%b-%Y", date_breaks = "3 months")
  
  # Final return 
  return(list(
    city = city_name,
    clean_df = df_clean,
    sac_with = sac_df,
    sac_without = sac_no_cnc,
    merged = merged_df,
    plot = p
  ))
}

# code to isolate blank records (Clare Collins)
user_records_hull <- inat_hull %>% dplyr::filter(user_id == 6464285)

# Datasets
results_list <- list(
  Hull = process_inat_city(inat_hull, "Hull"),
  Birmingham = process_inat_city(inat_birm, "Birmingham"),
  Liverpool = process_inat_city(inat_liver, "Liverpool"),
  Bristol = process_inat_city(inat_brist, "Bristol"),
  Brighton = process_inat_city(inat_brig, "Brighton"),
  York = process_inat_city(inat_york, "York"),
  Staffordshire = process_inat_city(inat_staff, "Staffordshire"),
  Cheshire = process_inat_city(inat_chesh, "Cheshire"),
  NorthEast = process_inat_city(inat_northeast, "North East"),
  London = process_inat_city(inat_london, "London"),
  Manchester = process_inat_city(inat_man, "Manchester"),
  Lancashire = process_inat_city(inat_lanc, "Lancashire"),
  Oxford = process_inat_city(inat_oxf, "Oxford"),
  Edinburgh = process_inat_city(inat_ed, "Edinburgh"),
  Warwick_and_Solihull = process_inat_city(inat_war_sol, "Warwick and Solihull"),
  Suffolk = process_inat_city(inat_suffolk, "Suffolk"),
  Belfast = process_inat_city(inat_belfast, "Belfast"),
  Berkshire = process_inat_city(inat_berk, "Berkshire"),
  Swansea = process_inat_city(inat_swansea, "Swansea"),
  Dundee = process_inat_city(inat_dundee, "Dundee"),
  Gloucestershire = process_inat_city(inat_gloucest, "Gloucestershire"),
  Coventry = process_inat_city(inat_coventry, "Coventry"),
  Cardiff = process_inat_city(inat_cardnew, "Cardiff and Newport"),
  Nottingham = process_inat_city(inat_nott, "Nottingham"),
  Glasgow = process_inat_city(inat_glas, "Glasgow")
)

# Plots
plots <- map(results_list, "plot")
results_list$Hull
results_list$Edinburgh
results_list$London
# Extract just the plots
plots_formatted <- map2(plots, names(plots), ~ .x +
  labs(title = .y, x = NULL, y = NULL) +   # remove axis titles
  scale_x_date(date_labels = "%b-%y", date_breaks = "1 year") +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(size = 12, face = "bold", hjust = 0.5),
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1, size = 12),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 12),
    axis.title.x = element_blank(),
    axis.title.y = element_blank()
  )
)

# Combine in a 2x5 grid
combined_sac <- wrap_plots(plots_formatted, ncol = 5, nrow = 5) +
  plot_annotation(
    title = "",
    subtitle = "Dashed = with CNC, solid = without CNC",
    theme = theme(
      plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 16, hjust = 0.5),
      plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 12, hjust = 0.5)
    )
  )

# Display
combined_sac

ggsave(
  filename = "combined_sac_A4.png",
  plot = combined_sac,
  width = 9, 
  height = 12,
  units = "in",
  dpi = 900
)
```

```{r Difference Curves}
datasets <- list(
  "Hull" = inat_hull,
  "Birmingham" = inat_birm,
  "Liverpool" = inat_liver,
  "Bristol" = inat_brist,
  "Brighton" = inat_brig,
  "Staffordshire" = inat_staff,
  "Cheshire" = inat_chesh,
  "York" = inat_york,
  "London" = inat_london,
  "North East" = inat_northeast,
  "Manchester" = inat_man,
  "Lancashire" = inat_lanc,
  "Oxfordshire" = inat_oxf,
  "Edinburgh" = inat_ed,
  "Warwick and Solihull" = inat_war_sol,
  "Suffolk" = inat_suffolk,
  "Belfast" = inat_belfast,
  "Berkshire" = inat_berk,
  "Swansea" = inat_swansea,
  "Dundee" = inat_dundee,
  "Gloucestershire" = inat_gloucest,
  "Coventry" = inat_coventry,
  "Cardiff" = inat_cardnew,
  "Nottingham" = inat_nott,
  "Glasgow" = inat_glas
)

# Apply the function to each dataset
results_list <- imap(datasets, process_inat_city)

# Combine differences into one dataframe
all_diffs <- map_dfr(results_list, ~ .x$merged %>% 
                       mutate(city = .x$city) %>% 
                       select(city, month, diff))

#
ggplot(all_diffs, aes(x = month, y = diff, color = city)) +
  geom_line(size = 1.2) +
  labs(
    x = "Month", 
    y = "Difference in Cumulative Species Richness",
    title = "CNC Impact on Species Accumulation by City",
    color = "City"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  scale_x_date(date_labels = "%b-%Y", date_breaks = "3 months") +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1, size = 12)
  )
```

```{r difference curves avearge}
library(lubridate)

event_months <- data.frame(
  month = as.Date(c(
    "2021-05-01",
    "2022-05-01",
    "2023-04-01",
    "2024-04-01",
    "2025-04-01"
  ))
)

# For each city, find the first month where CNC changes richness
all_diffs_active <- all_diffs %>%
  group_by(city) %>%
  mutate(
    first_active_month = if (any(diff > 0)) min(month[diff > 0]) else as.Date(NA),
    started = !is.na(first_active_month) & month >= first_active_month
  ) %>%
  ungroup()

# Monthly summary using *only* months after the project is "started"
monthly_summary <- all_diffs_active %>%
  group_by(month) %>%
  summarise(
    mean_diff = mean(diff, na.rm = TRUE),
    n_active  = n()
  ) %>%
  arrange(month) %>%
  mutate(mean_diff = mean_diff - first(mean_diff))   # ← baseline fix


##
avg_diff <- ggplot() +
  # faint individual city lines
  geom_line(
    data = all_diffs_active,
    aes(x = month, y = diff, group = city),
    colour = "grey80",
    alpha  = 0.6
  ) +

  # mean line
  geom_line(
    data = monthly_summary,
    aes(x = month, y = mean_diff),
    colour   = "black",
    linewidth = 1.1
  ) +
  
  # black dots (all months)
geom_point(
  data = event_months %>% 
           left_join(monthly_summary, by = c("month" = "month")),
  aes(x = month, y = mean_diff),
  colour = "#74ac00",
  size = 3
) +

  labs(
    x = "Month",
    y = "Difference in Cumulative Species Richness\n(with vs. without CNC)"
  ) +
  scale_x_date(
    date_breaks = "6 months",
    date_labels = "%b\n%Y"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1)
  )
avg_diff
# biological persistance of cnc at a national-scale
```

```{r difference curvers panel}
p_facets <- ggplot(all_diffs, aes(x = month, y = diff)) +
  geom_line(color = "steelblue", linewidth = 0.6) +
  facet_wrap(~ city, ncol = 5) +          # 5 x 5 grid (25 cities)
  labs(
    x = "Month",
    y = "Difference in Cumulative Species Richness\n(with vs. without CNC)",
    title = "CNC Impact on Species Accumulation by City"
  ) +
  scale_x_date(
    date_breaks = "1 year",
    date_labels = "%Y"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  theme(
    strip.text = element_text(face = "bold", size = 9),
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1, size = 14),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank()
  )

p_facets

ggsave(
  filename = "p_facets.png",
  plot = p_facets,
  width = 9, 
  height = 12,
  units = "in",
  dpi = 900,
  bg = "white"
)

```

```{r sanity data check}
# name check 
hull_results <- process_inat_city(inat_hull, "Hull")
tibble(
  city = "Hull",
  species = unique(hull_results$sac_with$month_species |> unlist())
)
species_hull <- tibble(species = unique(unlist(hull_results$sac_with$month_species)))
hybrids_hull <- species_hull %>%
  filter(str_detect(species, "×"))
length(unique(inat_hull$taxon_species_name))
length(unique(species_hull$species))

# Unique names before cleaning
all_names <- unique(inat_hull$scientific_name)

# Classify by number of words and simplify (check names have been handled correctly)
df_name_check <- tibble(
  original = all_names,
  n_words = str_count(all_names, "\\S+"),
  simplified = if_else(
    str_detect(all_names, "×"),
    all_names,
    word(all_names, 1, 2)
  )
)

# Genus-only (1 word)
genus_only <- df_name_check %>% filter(n_words == 1)

# Subspecies/variety collapsing (≥3 words collapsing into same simplified name)
collapsed <- df_name_check %>%
  filter(n_words > 2) %>%
  group_by(simplified) %>%
  summarise(
    n_sub_names = n_distinct(original),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  filter(n_sub_names > 1)

# Summary table
summary_tbl <- tibble(
  total_unique_before = length(all_names),
  genus_only = nrow(genus_only),
  collapsed_species = nrow(collapsed),
  total_unique_after = length(unique(df_name_check$simplified))
)
summary_tbl
```

```{r CNC impact per year}
# Function to summarise CNC effect per year
summarise_cnc_effect <- function(merged_df, city_name) {
  merged_df %>%
    mutate(year = lubridate::year(month)) %>%
    group_by(year) %>%
    summarise(
      # Skip year if no data available
      has_diff = any(!is.na(diff)),
      
      CNC_gain = if_else(
        has_diff,
        max(diff, na.rm = TRUE),      # Use the max difference (i.e, the end of the year)
        NA_real_                       # no comparison possible
      ),
      baseline_richness = if_else(
        any(!is.na(cum_species_without)),
        max(cum_species_without, na.rm = TRUE),
        NA_real_
      ),
      .groups = "drop"
    ) %>%
    mutate(
      percent_gain = if_else(
        is.na(CNC_gain) | is.na(baseline_richness),
        NA_real_,
        round((CNC_gain / baseline_richness) * 100, 2)
      ),
      city = city_name
    ) %>%
    select(city, year, CNC_gain, baseline_richness, percent_gain) %>%
    arrange(year)
}

# Apply function to each city and combine results
cnc_summary_all <- purrr::map_dfr(results_list, ~ summarise_cnc_effect(.x$merged, .x$city))

# View
print(cnc_summary_all)

# Lookup table for showing CNC years (data is viewed by participation year and not actual year in this analysis)
cnc_participation <- tibble(
  city = c("Hull", "Birmingham", "Liverpool", "Bristol", 
           "Brighton", "York", "Staffordshire", "Cheshire", "North East", "London", "Manchester", "Lancashire",
           "Oxfordshire", "Edinburgh", "Warwick and Solihull", "Suffolk" , "Belfast" , "Berkshire" , "Swansea" ,
           "Dundee" , "Gloucestershire" , "Coventry" , "Cardiff" , "Nottingham" , "Glasgow"),
  cnc_years = list(
    c(2023, 2024, 2025),              # Hull
    c(2020, 2021, 2022, 2023, 2024, 2025),  # Birmingham
    c(2019, 2020, 2021, 2022, 2023, 2024, 2025),  # Liverpool
    c(2018, 2019, 2020, 2021, 2022, 2023, 2024, 2025),  # Bristol
    c(2021, 2022, 2023, 2024, 2025),  # Brighton
    c(2024, 2025),                    # York
    c(2023, 2024, 2025),              # Staffordshire
    c(2023, 2024, 2025),              # Cheshire
    c(2019, 2020, 2021, 2022, 2023, 2024, 2025),  # North East
    c(2018, 2019, 2020, 2021, 2022, 2023, 2024, 2025),  # London
    c(2019, 2020, 2021, 2022, 2023, 2024, 2025),  # Manchester
    c(2020, 2021, 2022, 2024, 2025),        # Lancashire
    c(2024, 2025),                    # Oxfordshire
    c(2021, 2022, 2023, 2024, 2025),  # Greater Edinburgh
    c(2023, 2024),                    # Warwick and Solihull
    c(2023, 2024),                    # Suffolk
    c(2024, 2025),                    # Belfast
    c(2024, 2025),                    # Berkshire
    c(2023, 2024, 2025),              # Swansea
    c(2023, 2024, 2025),              # Dundee
    c(2021, 2022, 2023, 2024),        # Gloucestershire
    c(2021, 2022, 2023, 2024),        # Coventry
    c(2021, 2022, 2023, 2025),        # Cardiff and Newport
    c(2020, 2021, 2022, 2023, 2024, 2025),   # Nottingham
    c(2021, 2022, 2023, 2024, 2025)   # Glasgow
    )
)

#
cnc_summary_marked <- cnc_summary_all %>%
  left_join(cnc_participation, by = "city") %>%
  mutate(
    participation_year = purrr::map2_dbl(year, cnc_years, ~ match(.x, .y)),
    is_cnc_year = !is.na(participation_year)
  )

# Join the CNC participation info to summary table
cnc_summary_marked <- cnc_summary_all %>%
  left_join(cnc_participation, by = "city") %>%
  mutate(
    participation_year = purrr::map2_dbl(year, cnc_years, ~ match(.x, .y)),
    is_cnc_year = !is.na(participation_year)
  ) %>%
  group_by(city) %>%
  arrange(year, .by_group = TRUE) %>%
  mutate(
    prev_year_available = (year - 1) %in% year,
    unusable_cnc_year = is_cnc_year & !prev_year_available,
    CNC_gain     = if_else(unusable_cnc_year, NA_real_, CNC_gain),
    percent_gain = if_else(unusable_cnc_year, NA_real_, percent_gain)
  ) %>%
  ungroup()

# run plot
ggplot(
  cnc_summary_marked %>%
    filter(is_cnc_year, !is.na(percent_gain)),
  aes(x = participation_year, y = percent_gain, color = city, group = city)
) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1.2) +
  geom_point(size = 3) +
  labs(
    title = "CNC Contribution to Species Richness by Participation Year",
    x = "Participation year (since first CNC)",
    y = "Percent gain in species richness",
    color = "City"
  ) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = 1:10) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", hjust = 0.5),
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1)
  )
```

```{r cnc contribution per year}
cnc_year_summary <- cnc_summary_marked %>%
  filter(is_cnc_year, !is.na(percent_gain)) %>%
  arrange(city, year) %>%
  group_by(city) %>%
  mutate(
    cnc_participation_number = participation_year,
    year_label = paste0("CNC Year ", participation_year)
  ) %>%
  ungroup()

#
ggplot(cnc_year_summary, aes(x = cnc_participation_number, y = percent_gain, color = city, group = city)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1.2) +
  geom_point(size = 3) +
  scale_x_continuous(
    breaks = 1:5,
    labels = paste("Year", 1:5)
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Change in CNC Impact Across Participation Years",
    subtitle = "Does CNC Year 1 show the strongest biodiversity gain?",
    x = "CNC Participation Year (relative)",
    y = "Percent Gain in Species Richness",
    color = "City"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", hjust = 0.5),
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 0)
  )

# avg.
cnc_year_avg <- cnc_year_summary %>%
  group_by(cnc_participation_number) %>%
  summarise(
    mean_percent_gain = mean(percent_gain, na.rm = TRUE),
    sd_percent_gain = sd(percent_gain, na.rm = TRUE),
    n_cities = n()
  )

## # Ensure consistent x-scaling (continuous)
breaks_years <- cnc_year_avg$cnc_participation_number
labels_years <- paste("Year", breaks_years)

T1 <- ggplot(cnc_year_avg, aes(x = cnc_participation_number, y = mean_percent_gain)) +
  geom_line(color = "steelblue", linewidth = 1.3) +
  geom_point(size = 3, color = "darkorange") +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = mean_percent_gain - sd_percent_gain,
                    ymax = mean_percent_gain + sd_percent_gain),
                width = 0.1, alpha = 0.6) +
  scale_x_continuous(
    breaks = cnc_year_avg$cnc_participation_number,
    labels = paste("Year", cnc_year_avg$cnc_participation_number)
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Average CNC Impact Across Participation Years",
    subtitle = "Mean percent gain in species richness across all cities",
    x = "CNC Participation Year",
    y = "Average Percent Gain in Species Richness"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", hjust = 0.5)
  )

# option 2 
ggplot() +
  geom_line(data = cnc_year_summary, aes(x = cnc_participation_number, y = percent_gain, group = city),
            color = "grey80", alpha = 0.7) +
  geom_line(data = cnc_year_avg, aes(x = cnc_participation_number, y = mean_percent_gain),
            color = "steelblue", linewidth = 1.3) +
  geom_point(data = cnc_year_avg, aes(x = cnc_participation_number, y = mean_percent_gain),
             color = "darkorange", size = 3) +
  labs(
    title = "Average CNC Impact Across Participation Years",
    subtitle = "Thin grey lines = individual cities; blue line = mean trend",
    x = "CNC Participation Year",
    y = "Percent Gain in Species Richness"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13)

# Boxplot
T2 <- ggplot(cnc_year_summary, aes(x = factor(cnc_participation_number), y = percent_gain)) +
  # Boxplots show distribution across cities
  geom_boxplot(outlier.shape = NA, fill = "grey90", color = "grey40", width = 0.5) +
  
  # Jittered points show individual city results
  geom_jitter(aes(color = city), width = 0.15, alpha = 0.7, size = 2.5) +
  
  # Axis formatting
  scale_x_discrete(labels = paste("Year", cnc_year_avg$cnc_participation_number)) +
  labs(
    title = "",
    subtitle = "",
    x = "CNC Participation Year",
    y = "Percent Gain in Species Richness",
    color = "City"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", hjust = 0.5),
    axis.text.x = element_text(size = 12),
    legend.position = "none"  # optional, to declutter
  )
```

```{r annual declines model}
# models
mod_mixed <- lmer(percent_gain ~ cnc_participation_number + 
                  (cnc_participation_number | city), 
                  data = cnc_year_summary,
                  REML = FALSE)
summary(mod_mixed) # dont have enough data

## lm (linear)
mod_simple <- lmer(percent_gain ~ cnc_participation_number + (1 | city),
                   data = cnc_year_summary,
                   REML = FALSE)

summary(mod_simple) # good
residuals(mod_simple)
deviance(mod_simple)
plot(mod_simple)                          # residuals vs fitted
sim_res <- simulateResiduals(mod_simple, n = 1000)
plot(sim_res)
testOutliers(sim_res)

## non-linear (not better with the data available)
mod_nonlinear <- lmer(
  percent_gain ~ poly(cnc_participation_number, 2, raw = TRUE) + (1 | city),
  data = cnc_year_summary,
  REML = FALSE
)
summary(mod_nonlinear)

# Diagnostics
plot(mod_nonlinear)
sim_res <- simulateResiduals(mod_nonlinear, n = 1000) # significant effects
plot(sim_res)
testOutliers(sim_res)
```

```{r annual declines model - plot}
# Packages needed for colours + compressing y axis
library(viridis)  # if not already loaded
library(ggbreak)


# Build prediction grid (numeric)
newdat <- data.frame(
  cnc_participation_number = 1:8,
  city = NA
)

# Predict from the mixed model (numeric predictor)
pred <- predict(
  mod_simple,
  newdata = newdat,
  se.fit = TRUE,
  re.form = NA
)

newdat$fit   <- pred$fit
newdat$se    <- pred$se.fit
newdat$lower <- newdat$fit - 1.96 * newdat$se
newdat$upper <- newdat$fit + 1.96 * newdat$se


# Plot — convert x to factor ONLY inside ggplot

# Copy model CI to plotting CI (safe)
newdat$lower_plot <- pmax(newdat$lower, 0)
newdat$upper_plot <- pmin(newdat$upper, 40)

participation_yr <- ggplot(
  cnc_year_summary,
  aes(x = factor(cnc_participation_number), y = percent_gain)
) +
  geom_jitter(
    color = "grey50",
    width = 0.15,
    size = 2,
    alpha = 0.6
  ) +
  geom_ribbon(
    data = newdat,
    inherit.aes = FALSE,
    aes(
      x = factor(cnc_participation_number),
      ymin = lower_plot,
      ymax = upper_plot,
      group = 1
    ),
    fill = "#74ac00",
    alpha = 0.25
  ) +
  geom_line(
    data = newdat,
    inherit.aes = FALSE,
    aes(
      x = factor(cnc_participation_number),
      y = fit,
      group = 1
    ),
    color = "#74ac00",
    linewidth = 1.2
  ) +
  
  # Y-axis with break between 20 and 35
  scale_y_continuous(
    limits = c(0, 40),
    breaks = c(0, 5, 10, 15, 20, 35, 40)
  ) +
  scale_y_break(c(20, 35), ticklabels = c(20, 35, 40)) +
  
  labs(
    x = "CNC Participation Year",
    y = "Percent Gain in Species Richness"
  ) +
  
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
  theme(
    legend.position = "none",
    axis.text.y.right = element_blank(),
    axis.ticks.y.right = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.major.x = element_blank()
  )

participation_yr

# table
cnc_year_stats <- cnc_year_summary %>%
  group_by(cnc_participation_number) %>%
  summarise(
    n = n(),
    mean = round(mean(percent_gain, na.rm = TRUE), 2),
    sd   = round(sd(percent_gain, na.rm = TRUE), 2),
    min  = round(min(percent_gain, na.rm = TRUE), 2),
    max  = round(max(percent_gain, na.rm = TRUE), 2)
  )
common_theme <- theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
  theme(
    axis.title = element_text(size = 12),
    axis.text  = element_text(size = 10),
    plot.title = element_text(size = 13, face = "bold", hjust = 0.5),
    legend.title = element_text(size = 11),
    legend.text  = element_text(size = 9),
    plot.margin  = margin(5, 10, 5, 10)
  )
  
  combined_fig <- avg_diff / participation_yr +
  plot_layout(heights = c(1, 1))  # top panel a bit taller

combined_fig

ggsave(
  filename = "cnc_contribution.png",
  plot = combined_fig,
  width = 10, 
  height = 10,
  units = "in",
  dpi = 400
)
```

```{r unique cnc taxa}
# process_cnc_city
process_cnc_city <- function(city_results) {
  city_name <- city_results$city
  message("Processing CNC summary for ", city_name, "...")

# Extract the cleaned data (already research grade + simplified)
inat_clean <- city_results$clean_df

# CNC weekend date ranges
cnc_ranges <- list(
    "2021" = as.Date(c("2021-04-30", "2021-05-03")),
    "2022" = as.Date(c("2022-04-29", "2022-05-02")),
    "2023" = as.Date(c("2023-04-28", "2023-05-01")),
    "2024" = as.Date(c("2024-04-26", "2024-04-29")),
    "2025" = as.Date(c("2025-04-25", "2025-04-28"))
)

# Separate CNC vs non-CNC observations
inat_no_cnc <- inat_clean %>%
  filter(
    !(date >= cnc_ranges[["2021"]][1] & date <= cnc_ranges[["2021"]][2]) &
    !(date >= cnc_ranges[["2022"]][1] & date <= cnc_ranges[["2022"]][2]) &
    !(date >= cnc_ranges[["2023"]][1] & date <= cnc_ranges[["2023"]][2]) &
    !(date >= cnc_ranges[["2024"]][1] & date <= cnc_ranges[["2024"]][2]) &
    !(date >= cnc_ranges[["2025"]][1] & date <= cnc_ranges[["2025"]][2])
    )

cnc_observations <- anti_join(inat_clean, inat_no_cnc, by = "id")

# CNC-unique species (species not seen outside CNC)
  cnc_unique <- cnc_observations %>%
    anti_join(inat_no_cnc, by = "scientific_name") %>%
    select(scientific_name, iconic_taxon_name, user_id, date, month) %>%
    add_count(scientific_name, name = "obs_count") %>%
    distinct(scientific_name, .keep_all = TRUE) %>%
    arrange(desc(obs_count), iconic_taxon_name, scientific_name)

#  Rename to user friendly names
cnc_unique <- cnc_unique %>%
    mutate(
      taxon_group = case_when(
        iconic_taxon_name %in% c("Reptilia", "Amphibia") ~ "Reptiles and Amphibians",
        iconic_taxon_name %in% c("Chromista", "Protozoa") ~ "Protozoa and Chromista",
        iconic_taxon_name == "Animalia" ~ "Other Animalia",
        iconic_taxon_name == "Actinopterygii" ~ "Fishes",
        iconic_taxon_name == "Aves" ~ "Birds",
        iconic_taxon_name == "Plantae" ~ "Plants",
        iconic_taxon_name == "Fungi" ~ "Fungus",
        iconic_taxon_name == "Insecta" ~ "Insects",
        iconic_taxon_name == "Arachnida" ~ "Spiders",
        iconic_taxon_name == "Mollusca" ~ "Molluscs",
        iconic_taxon_name == "Mammalia" ~ "Mammals",
        TRUE ~ iconic_taxon_name
      )
    )

# Taxonomic summary
taxonomic_summary <- cnc_unique %>%
  count(taxon_group, sort = TRUE) %>%
  mutate(percentage = round(n / sum(n) * 100, 1)) %>%
  filter(!is.na(taxon_group) & taxon_group != "Unknown")

# Summary statistics
  stats <- tibble(
    city = city_name,
    total_cnc_unique_species = nrow(cnc_unique),
    single_obs = sum(cnc_unique$obs_count == 1),
    two_to_five = sum(cnc_unique$obs_count %in% 2:5),
    six_plus = sum(cnc_unique$obs_count >= 6)
  )

# Return structured list
  list(
    city = city_name,
    cnc_unique = cnc_unique,
    taxonomic_summary = taxonomic_summary,
    summary_stats = stats
  )
}

# Process CNC summaries for all cities

# Process all cities through your main inat processing function
results_list <- imap(datasets, process_inat_city)

# Apply CNC summary function to each processed result
cnc_results <- map(results_list, process_cnc_city)

# Combine CNC summary stats into one data frame
cnc_summary_all <- map_dfr(cnc_results, ~ .x$summary_stats)

# Combine taxonomic breakdowns for all cities
cnc_taxa_all <- map_dfr(
  cnc_results,
  ~ .x$taxonomic_summary %>%
    mutate(city = .x$city)
)

# Summary CNC-unique species
print(cnc_summary_all)

# select colours for groups
taxon_group <- c(
  "Birds"                   = "#E76F51",
  "Mammals"                 = "#2A9D8F",
  "Reptiles and Amphibians" = "#52B788",
  "Fungus"                  = "#F4A261",
  "Plants"                  = "#74ac00AA",
  "Insects"                 = "#93914c",
  "Spiders"                 = "#E69A8D",
  "Molluscs"                = "#457B9D",
  "Fishes"                  = "#00B4D8",
  "Other Animalia"          = "#ADB5BD",
  "Protozoa and Chromista"  = "#B56576"
)

# Calculate total unique species per city and define order
city_totals <- cnc_taxa_all %>%
  group_by(city) %>%
  summarise(total_n = sum(n, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") %>%
  arrange(desc(total_n))

city_order <- city_totals$city 

# Unique species totals bar plot
p1 <- ggplot(city_totals, aes(x = fct_reorder(city, -total_n), y = total_n)) +
  geom_col(fill = "grey60") +
  labs(
    x = "",
    y = "Number of CNC-unique species",
    title = ""
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_blank(),
    axis.ticks.x = element_blank(),
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold"),
    
    # 🔥 Remove vertical gridlines
    panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor.x = element_blank()
  )

# Stacked bar
p2 <- ggplot(
  cnc_taxa_all %>% mutate(city = factor(city, levels = city_order)),
  aes(x = city, y = n, fill = taxon_group)
) +
  geom_col(position = "fill") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = taxon_group) +
  labs(
    x = "",
    y = "Proportion of CNC-unique species",
    fill = "Taxonomic group"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(
      angle = 90,
      hjust = 1,
      vjust = 0.5        # <-- fixes label gap
    ),
    plot.margin = margin(t = -5, r = 10, b = 0, l = 10),
     panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(),
     panel.grid.minor.x = element_blank(),
     panel.grid.minor.y = element_blank(),
     panel.grid.major.y = element_blank(),
    legend.position = "bottom",
    legend.title = element_text(size = 13, face = "bold"),
    legend.text  = element_text(size = 11)
  )

# combine
combined_plot <- p1 / p2 +
  plot_layout(heights = c(1, 2))  # bottom plot taller

# view
```

```{r unique cnc taxa}
combined_plot
combined_plot <- combined_plot &
  theme(
    axis.title = element_text(size = 13),
    axis.text  = element_text(size = 14),
    strip.text = element_text(size = 14),
    plot.title = element_text(size = 18, face = "bold"),
    plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 14)
  )
ggsave(
  filename = "cnc_species_comp.png",
  plot = combined_plot,
  width = 10, 
  height = 10,
  units = "in",
  dpi = 400
)
```

```{r sliding window}
inat_all <- bind_rows(
  mutate(inat_hull, city = "Hull"),
  mutate(inat_birm, city = "Birmingham"),
  mutate(inat_liver, city = "Liverpool"),
  mutate(inat_brist, city = "Bristol"),
  mutate(inat_brig, city = "Brighton"),
  mutate(inat_york, city = "York"),
  mutate(inat_staff, city = "Staffordshire"),
  mutate(inat_chesh, city = "Cheshire"),
  mutate(inat_northeast, city = "North East"),
  mutate(inat_london, city = "London"),
  mutate(inat_man, city = "Manchester"),
  mutate(inat_lanc, city = "Lancashire"),
  mutate(inat_oxf, city = "Oxfordshire"),
  mutate(inat_ed, city = "Edinburgh"),
  mutate(inat_war_sol, city = "Warwick and Solihull"),
  mutate(inat_suffolk, city = "Suffolk"),
  mutate(inat_belfast, city = "Belfast"),
  mutate(inat_berk, city = "Berkshire"),
  mutate(inat_swansea, city = "Swansea"),
  mutate(inat_dundee, city = "Dundee"),
  mutate(inat_gloucest, city = "Gloucestershire"),
  mutate(inat_coventry, city = "Coventry"),
  mutate(inat_cardnew, city = "Cardiff"),
  mutate(inat_nott, city = "Nottingham"),
  mutate(inat_glas, city = "Glasgow")
)

inat_all <- inat_all %>%
  mutate(
    observed_on = as.Date(observed_on),
    year = year(observed_on)
  )

cnc_participation_long <- cnc_participation %>% unnest(cnc_years)

inat_all <- inat_all %>%
  inner_join(cnc_participation_long, by = c("city", "year" = "cnc_years")) %>%
  mutate(
    cnc_start = case_when(
      year == 2021 ~ as.Date("2021-04-30"),
      year == 2022 ~ as.Date("2022-04-29"),
      year == 2023 ~ as.Date("2023-04-28"),
      year == 2024 ~ as.Date("2024-04-26"),
      year == 2025 ~ as.Date("2025-04-25"),
      TRUE ~ NA_Date_
    ),
    weeks_from_cnc = floor(as.numeric(difftime(observed_on, cnc_start, units = "days")) / 7)
  )

# Weekly user counts
weekly_users <- inat_all %>%
  filter(between(weeks_from_cnc, -5, 5)) %>%
  group_by(city, year, weeks_from_cnc) %>%
  summarise(unique_users = n_distinct(user_id), .groups = "drop")

# Mean ± SE per city
city_summary <- weekly_users %>%
  group_by(city, weeks_from_cnc) %>%
  summarise(
    mean_users = mean(unique_users, na.rm = TRUE),
    sd_users   = sd(unique_users, na.rm = TRUE),
    n_years    = n(),
    se_users   = sd_users / sqrt(n_years),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

# Absolute engagement
ggplot(city_summary, aes(x = weeks_from_cnc, y = mean_users)) +
  geom_col(fill = "skyblue") +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = mean_users - se_users, ymax = mean_users + se_users),
                width = 0.3, color = "gray30") +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, color = "red", linetype = "dashed") +
  facet_wrap(~ city, scales = "free_y", ncol = 4) +
  labs(x = "Weeks relative to CNC", y = "Mean unique users (±SE)") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12)

# Relative engagement
city_summary <- city_summary %>%
  group_by(city) %>%
  mutate(
    baseline = mean(mean_users[weeks_from_cnc < 0], na.rm = TRUE),
    rel_users = (mean_users / baseline) * 100,
    rel_se = (se_users / baseline) * 100,
    max_rel = max(rel_users, na.rm = TRUE)  # for ordering
  ) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  mutate(city = fct_reorder(city, -max_rel))

ggplot(city_summary, aes(x = weeks_from_cnc, y = rel_users)) +
  # highlight CNC week without warning
  annotate("rect",
           xmin = -0.5, xmax = 0.5, ymin = -Inf, ymax = Inf,
           fill = "grey90", alpha = 0.5) +
  geom_col(fill = "steelblue") +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = rel_users - rel_se, ymax = rel_users + rel_se),
                width = 0.25, color = "gray30") +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, color = "red", linetype = "dashed") +
  facet_wrap(~ city, ncol = 4) +
  coord_cartesian(ylim = c(0, 450)) +
  labs(
    title = "Weekly Engagement Relative to the City Nature Challenge",
    subtitle = "Mean engagement (± SE) expressed as % of pre-CNC baseline",
    x = "Weeks relative to CNC",
    y = "Engagement (% of pre-CNC mean ± SE)"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
  theme(
    strip.text = element_text(face = "bold", size = 11),
    panel.spacing = unit(1.2, "lines"),
    axis.text.x = element_text(size = 9),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 9),
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", hjust = 0.5, size = 15),
    plot.subtitle = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 12)
  )
```

```{r model for engagement (exploration)}
# create cnc variable
city_summary <- city_summary %>%
  mutate(
    is_cnc_week = if_else(weeks_from_cnc == 0, 1, 0)
  )
#log
city_summary <- city_summary %>%
  mutate(log_rel_users = log(rel_users))

# diagnostics
hist(city_summary$rel_users)
hist(log(city_summary$rel_users))

# fixed model
m_fixed <- lm(rel_users ~ is_cnc_week, data = city_summary)
summary(m_fixed)

#log
m_log <- lm(log_rel_users ~ is_cnc_week, data = city_summary)
summary(m_log)


# diagnostics of two models
par(mfrow = c(2, 2))
plot(m_fixed)
par(mfrow = c(1, 1))

par(mfrow = c(2, 2))
plot(m_log)
par(mfrow = c(1, 1))
```

```{r polynomial model to account for seasonal increase in engagement}
# CNC Engagement

# Fit quadratic model
m_poly <- lm(rel_users ~ is_cnc_week + poly(weeks_from_cnc, 2), data = city_summary)
summary(m_poly)
par(mfrow = c(2, 2))
plot(m_poly)
par(mfrow = c(1, 1))
# Create smooth prediction data
newdata <- expand.grid(
  weeks_from_cnc = seq(min(city_summary$weeks_from_cnc), max(city_summary$weeks_from_cnc), length.out = 200),
  is_cnc_week = 0
)

# Predict with 95% CI
pred_poly <- cbind(
  newdata,
  predict(m_poly, newdata, interval = "confidence")
)

# Make the plot
user_plot <- ggplot() +
  
  # Raw data (city-level points)
  geom_point(
    data = city_summary,
    aes(x = weeks_from_cnc, y = rel_users),
    alpha = 0.5, color = "grey60", size = 2
  ) +
  
  # Confidence ribbon (use inherit.aes = FALSE)
  geom_ribbon(
    data = pred_poly,
    aes(x = weeks_from_cnc, ymin = lwr, ymax = upr),
    fill = "#74ac00AA", alpha = 0.5, inherit.aes = FALSE
  ) +
  
  # Fitted polynomial line
  geom_line(
    data = pred_poly,
    aes(x = weeks_from_cnc, y = fit),
    color = "black", linewidth = 0.8
  ) +
  
  # CNC week vertical line
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "solid", color = "grey60", linewidth = 0.6) +
  
  # Highlight CNC week points
  geom_point(
    data = filter(city_summary, weeks_from_cnc == 0),
    aes(x = weeks_from_cnc, y = rel_users),
    color = "#74ac00", size = 3, alpha = 0.6
  ) +
  
  # Labels and theme
  labs(
    title = "",
    subtitle = "",
    x = "Weeks relative to CNC",
    y = "Engagement Increase Compared to Baseline (%)"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", hjust = 0.5, size = 15),
    plot.subtitle = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 12),
    axis.title = element_text(size = 13),
    axis.text = element_text(size = 12),
    axis.line.x = element_line(color = "darkgrey", size = 0.8),
    axis.ticks.x = element_line(color = "darkgrey", size = 0.8)
    )
user_plot

#
ggsave(
  filename = "user_increase_A4.png",
  plot = user_plot,
  width = 8, 
  height = 6,
  units = "in",
  bg = "white",
  dpi = 400
)
```

```{r unique taxa code}
library(dplyr)
library(MASS)

# --- NEW MODELLING CHUNK (safe to append at end) ---

# 1. Create city-level baseline and CNC user metrics
baseline_users <- weekly_users %>%
  filter(weeks_from_cnc %in% -5:-1) %>%
  group_by(city) %>%
  summarise(baseline_users = mean(unique_users, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop")

cnc_users <- weekly_users %>%
  filter(weeks_from_cnc %in% 0:1) %>%
  group_by(city) %>%
  summarise(cnc_users = mean(unique_users, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop")

# 2. Compute correct city-level rel_users
city_user_metrics <- baseline_users %>%
  left_join(cnc_users, by = "city") %>%
  mutate(
    rel_users = cnc_users / baseline_users   # *ratio*, not weekly %
  )

# 3. Build modelling dataset (one row per city)
model_df <- city_totals %>%                       # contains total_n or unique taxa
  left_join(inat_urb %>% rename(city = City), by = "city") %>%  # per_urban
  left_join(city_user_metrics, by = "city") %>%   # rel_users, baseline_users, cnc_users
  mutate(
    CNC_area_km2 = CNC_area / 1e6
  )

#
model_df <- model_df %>%
  dplyr::select(city, total_n, rel_users, per_urban, CNC_area_km2) %>%
  tidyr::drop_na()


# 4. Negative Binomial models
library(MASS)

m_nb <- glm.nb(total_n ~ rel_users + per_urban + CNC_area_km2,
               data = model_df)
summary(m_nb)
sim_nb <- simulateResiduals(m_nb)
plot(sim_nb)
plot(sim_nb, quantreg = FALSE) # suitable for small sample

# Optional: interaction model
m_nb_int <- glm.nb(total_n ~ rel_users * per_urban + CNC_area_km2,
                   data = model_df)

AIC(m_nb, m_nb_int)
summary(m_nb_int)

# model checks
library(corrplot)

num_vars <- model_df %>%
  dplyr::select(rel_users, per_urban, CNC_area_km2)

cor_matrix <- cor(num_vars, use = "pairwise.complete.obs")
print(cor_matrix)

corrplot(
  cor_matrix,
  method = "color",
  type = "lower",
  tl.col = "black",
  addCoef.col = "black"  # 👈 adds numbers
)

library(car)
vif(m_nb)
pearson_resid <- residuals(m_nb, type = "pearson")
dispersion <- sum(pearson_resid^2) / m_nb$df.residual
dispersion

#
model_df$pred  <- predict(m_nb, type = "response")
model_df$resid <- residuals(m_nb, type = "pearson")

ggplot(model_df, aes(x = pred, y = resid)) +
  geom_point(size = 3, colour = "black") +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, colour = "red", linetype = "dashed") +
  labs(x = "Fitted values",
       y = "Pearson residuals",
       title = "Residuals vs Fitted Values") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13)

#
cooks <- cooks.distance(m_nb)

plot(cooks, type = "h", main = "Cook's distance")
abline(h = 4/nrow(model_df), col = "red", lty = 2)

#
ggplot(model_df, aes(x = pred, y = total_n)) +
  geom_point(size = 3, colour = "darkred") +
  geom_abline(slope = 1, intercept = 0, linetype = "dashed") +
  labs(x = "Predicted", y = "Observed",
       title = "Observed vs Predicted Values (NB Model)") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13)

#
library(pscl)
pR2(m_nb)
```

```{r unique taxa code}
library(effects)

eff_rel <- effect("rel_users", m_nb, 
                  xlevels = list(rel_users = seq(min(model_df$rel_users),
                                                 max(model_df$rel_users), 
                                                 length = 100)))

eff_rel_df <- as.data.frame(eff_rel)

# Plot
p_rel <- ggplot(eff_rel_df, aes(x = rel_users, y = fit)) +
  geom_line(size = 1.2, colour = "#74ac00") +
  geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = lower, ymax = upper), alpha = 0.2, fill = "#74ac00") +
  labs(
    x = "Relative engagement increase (CNC / baseline)",
    y = "Total number of unique records",
    title = ""
  ) +
  theme_bw(base_size = 13) +
  theme(
    plot.title   = element_text(size = 20, face = "bold", hjust = 0.5),

    # --- axis title margins ---
    axis.title.x = element_text(
      size = 18,
      margin = margin(t = 12)  # move x-axis title downward
    ),
    axis.title.y = element_text(
      size = 18,
      margin = margin(r = 20)  # move y-axis title leftward
    ),

    # --- axis tick label size ---
    axis.text    = element_text(size = 18),

    # --- overall plot margins (optional) ---
    plot.margin = margin(t = 15, r = 15, b = 15, l = 15)
  )
p_rel
#
ggsave(
  filename = "relative_engagement_effect_A4.png",
  plot = p_rel,
  width = 11.69, 
  height = 8.27,
  units = "in",
  dpi = 400
)
```
